Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Simple This scenario involves a skilled threat actor targeting healthcare and public health organizations using a simple attack chain involving spearphishing, remote file copy, command execution, and data exfiltration.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Web server services such as emails and databases, Shared DB or directory, Authentication issues
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Phishing, Web, Vulnerability exploit, Malware

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2022-28810 [CWE-94]: Improper Input Validation in Microsoft Office Word
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 511 - Infiltration of Software Development Environment
stage.description: The attacker sends a targeted phishing email with a malicious attachment exploiting a vulnerability in Microsoft Office Word to gain initial access to the target organization.

stage.name: Execution
stage.surface: Web server services such as emails and databases
stage.kev: CVE-2024-29988 [CWE-918]: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-Site Scripting')
stage.description: Upon successful login, the attacker copies a malicious file to the target system via a web-based interface, exploiting a Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attacker executes the copied malicious file using a command interpreter, exploiting an OS command injection vulnerability to establish persistence and execute additional payloads.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 24 - Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: After gaining unauthorized access to sensitive data, the attacker exfiltrates it from the compromised system by overwhelming the filter with overly large transactions.

Scenario.summary: This simple attack scenario demonstrates how a skilled threat actor can exploit multiple vulnerabilities to gain initial access, establish persistence, and ultimately exfiltrate sensitive data from a healthcare organization.
